name: James Blood Ulmer
description: American jazz and blues guitarist and singer
abstract: Willie James "Blood" Ulmer (February 8, 1940 – June 3, 2026) was an American jazz, free funk and blues guitarist and singer. He played a Gibson Byrdland guitar.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: A Tribute to Nina Simone
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Honor: Blues, Jazz, Rhythm and Blues, Soul, and Beyond
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Lisa Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: actually no, it’s a co-led album with Jamaaladeen Tacuma, Roland Shannon Jackson, Ornette, and Bern Nix on guitar? No, I’m sorry, James Blood Ulmer on guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lisa Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: David Murray
about_quotes.evidence: I could take that and use Ornette Coleman’s concept and James Blood Ulmer, they say jazz is the teacher and Funk is the preacher.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Murray interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 18, 2003
